summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

title: Sustaining Agile Operations
summary: This report analyzes emerging energy and water technologies that could improve operational agility in contested environments. It evaluates advances in portable power, storage, and purification to enhance sustainability and reduce logistics risk.

title: The U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Set Off Tremors. Is It Still Alive?
summary: The announcement of a U.S. deal to provide Saudi Arabia with nuclear technology has raised alarm about proliferation, especially in Israel. The Trump administration’s swift move to add conditions puts it in limbo.

summary: China might have taken greater advantage of the Strait of Hormuz closure to accelerate clean energy exports, but other exports led Beijing to moderate oil price rises.

title: The Nuanced Relationship Between Public Opinion and Foreign Policy
summary: Public sentiment is something decision-makers must contend with, especially in democratic states. However, the extent to which public opinion shapes foreign policy varies. Three Council of Councils experts in France, India, and Nigeria reflect on how public opinion does—and sometimes does not—affect foreign policy decisions.

title: A New Series of CFR Playbooks Outlines How U.S. Policymakers Can Navigate Foreign Policy Crisis Scenarios
summary: In a new collection of reports from the Wachenheim Center for Peace and Security at the Council on Foreign Relations (CFR), authors Peter J. Quaranto, Cecily Brewer, F. title: American Crisis Management for a New Era
summary: The United States lacks a rigorous, routinized approach to managing foreign policy crises. The Wachenheim Center for Peace and Security has developed a set of Crisis Response Playbooks and a Crisis Management Checklist to serve as quick-reference guides for policy planners during the immediate pre-crisis and active crisis phases, when bandwidth is limited and decisions are most consequential. The playbooks are...

title: Trump, Taiwan, and the Reagan Doctrine
summary: Every few months, critics proclaim that Donald Trump has “gone soft” on China over Taiwan. Their latest evidence is an alleged pause in a second major arms package ahead of Xi Jinping’s expected visit to Washington this fall—it is “alleged” because the U.S. government has never officially announced, let alone notified Taipei of such a pause.

title: Anja Manuel: Aspen Security Forum 2026 | Day 3
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Anja Manuel is part of a panel discussion concerning the shifting balance of power in the Indo-Pacific, rising regional tensions, the future of US alliances and partnerships, and the diplomatic, military, and economic tools needed to preserve stability.

title: Anja Manuel: Aspen Security Forum 2026 | Day 2
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Anja Manuel has a wide-ranging conversation on US trade policy, economic security, strategic competition, and the growing intersection between national security and the global economy.

title: Mamdani Is ‘Not Even Making Any Effort To Disguise His Prejudices’ | Victor Davis Hanson
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson weighs in on Mamdani's call to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he visits New York, and then Hanson discusses reports of an attempted meeting involving Iran's ambassador and a member of Mamdani's administration.

title: HR McMaster Says US Counterintelligence Is ‘Way Behind’
summary: Hoover Institution fellow H.R. McMaster says that Washington’s focus on terrorism allowed China and Russia to expand espionage, cyber, and influence operations while exploiting US technology and investment.

title: The magnificent ten: A coalition of the willing to drive the EU’s next enlargement
summary: A Team Enlargement coalition of EU member states can drive the changes needed to bring Ukraine and others into the bloc.

title: One foot out: Understanding Bulgaria’s geopolitical shift under Radev
summary: Rumen Radev is steering Bulgaria towards a more transactional foreign policy. The EU needs to engage with Sofia and push back on such hedging to secure future European cohesion

title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
summary: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

title: Trump allies are poised to weaken protections for historic sites — not just in D.C.
summary: A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.

summary: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.

summary: President Donald Trump is set to rally in Michigan next week as early in-person voting begins in the battleground state's primary, where voters will choose nominees for open Senate and governor's races.

summary: President Donald Trump is going ahead with new double-digit tariffs on dozens of U.S. trading partners just as the clock runs out Friday on stopgap levies he imposed after a stinging defeat at the Supreme Court.

title: Trump threatens 'major military punishment' after Houthi attacks on tankers in Red Sea
summary: International oil prices topped $100 a barrel after Houthi rebels in Yemen claimed they attacked two oil tankers in the Red Sea, raising new fears of disruptions to global energy supplies. In response, President Trump warned that if the Houthis attack another ship, the U.S. would inflict "major military punishment." He also warned of escalation inside Iran. Nick Schifrin reports.

title: Former U.S. diplomat breaks down Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping
summary: For perspective on the Houthis' efforts to block shipping in the Red Sea, Amna Nawaz spoke with Timothy Lenderking. During his decades-long career as a U.S. diplomat, he served as special envoy to Yemen and as acting assistant secretary for Near East Affairs. He's now at the law firm Squire Patton Boggs.

title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool
summary: Chinese policy around the use of their SPR as a geoeconomic tool, particularly in crisis scenarios, is not well understood. This project investigates potential ways for China to do so, but not whether China actually intends to use it in this way.

title: Slowing Data Center Construction Signals the Need for Pro-Growth Reforms
summary: America’s artificial intelligence boom is running into roadblocks. Bloomberg reports that half of all data-center construction plans have been canceled or delayed at the beginning of 2026. This slowing growth is due in part to local building moratoriums, tax code phaseouts, and trouble connecting to an increasingly bottlenecked grid. As demand for data centers increases, these supply constraints could make...

title: June's Inflation Relief Was a Peace Dividend
summary: American families finally saw some financial relief in June as prices at the pump fell. Those lower fuel prices pulled down the consumer price index (CPI) by 0.4% in June, the largest monthly decline since April 2020. But don’t pop the champagne corks just yet, because that improvement has started reversing this month. Alas, June’s lower prices weren’t the triumph of sound monetary or fiscal policy, but a peace...

title: Four Defense Export Reforms for the United States
summary: The global threat environment that the United States and its allies face in 2026 is darker than at any point since the Cold War, with military conflicts on three continents and menacing operations by Russia and the increasingly capable Chinese military. But it is the major munitions expenditure in these current conflicts that is perhaps most concerning. Insufficient magazine depth is forcing the United States and...

title: This Should Be the Next Enforcement Tool To Secure America’s Borders
summary: Praise is due to a recent Trump administration push to nail immigration lawyers for the broadly undeterred crime of running mass asylum fraud schemes, which have let huge volumes of ineligible illegal aliens into the country. summary: Whether you’re heading off on holiday or staying home this summer, ECFR’s entertainment recommendations—spanning books, podcasts and tv series—will help you reconnect with your internal and external wellbeing

title: Managed disorder: The hard road to a modest peace with Russia after Ukraine
summary: When the fighting in Ukraine stops, America and Europe will likely end up facing Russia in a low-grade rivalry for years to come. Here is how to prevent that disordered rivalry from sliding into another war

title: Living with Wet Basements
summary: This report presents findings from a study conducted in Pittsburgh to document the prevalence of and experiences with basement wetness, the barriers that residents face in addressing it, and options available to solve the problem.

title: Examining the Impact of Senate Bill 1160 on Utilization Review and Medical Treatment in California Workers' Compensation
summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Securing AI Algorithmic Insights
summary: In this report, the authors extend a security framework to provide the first systematic roadmap for protecting algorithmic insights, the novel techniques, methods, and design know-how that materially improve artificial intelligence systems.

title: AllHere Chatbot Scandal Shows How Not to Deploy AI
summary: Want proof that policymakers and school staff need help with artificial intelligence? Look no further than the Los Angeles Unified School District. Superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigned June 21 as the district’s superintendent amid an FBI probe that media reports have linked to a failed contract for an AI chatbot. The episode is a case study of the need for greater transparency in public schools and the dangers...

title: National Security Strategy (Joint Committee): Deterrence in an age of Russian aggression
summary: Armida van Rij gave evidence to UK Parliament's Joint Committee on the National Security Strategy for its inquiry into deterrence in an age of Russian aggression. View the whole committee meeting here . Armida van Rij View multimedia 13 July 2026

Volgens Tordoir beleeft de wereldeconomie een nieuwe 'China Shock 2.0'. title: Unpacking Europe: The path to EU enlargement
summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement. They took a detailed look at new and old ideas recently put forward, including a so-called associate membership for Ukraine, as well as at progress by candidate countries. title: Navy LPD-17 Flight II and LHA Amphibious Ship Programs: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The Navy is procuring two types of larger amphibious ships: LHA-type “big-deck” amphibious assault ships and LPD-17 Flight II class amphibious ships. Both types are built by Huntington Ingalls Industries/Ingalls Shipbuilding (HII/Ingalls) of Pascagoula, MS. Four LPD-17 Flight II ships were procured in FY2018-FY2025. The Navy’s FY2026 budget requests the procurement of the fifth LPD-17 Flight II class ship and...

title: Navy DDG-51 and DDG-1000 Destroyer Programs: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The Arleigh Burke (DDG-51) class destroyer program is one of the longest-running shipbuilding programs in Navy history. The Navy began procuring DDG-51s, also known as Aegis destroyers, in FY1985, and a total of 97 have been procured through FY2025, including three in FY2025. From FY1989 through FY2005, DDG-51s were procured in annual quantities of two to five ships per year. Since FY2010, they have been procured...

title: Navy Ford (CVN-78) Class Aircraft Carrier Program: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The aircraft carriers CVN-78, CVN-79, CVN-80, CVN-81, CVN-82, and CVN-83 are the first six ships in the Navy’s new Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) class of nuclear-powered aircraft carriers (CVNs). The Navy’s proposed FY2026 budget requests $3,431.6 million (i.e., about $3.4 billion) in advance procurement (AP), procurement, and cost-to-complete (CTC) funding for Ford-class ships, as detailed below. CVN-78 (Gerald R. Ford)...

title: Navy Ship Names: Background for Congress
summary: Names for Navy ships traditionally have been chosen and announced by the Secretary of the Navy, under the direction of the President and in accordance with rules prescribed by Congress. Rules for giving certain types of names to certain types of Navy ships have evolved over time. There have been exceptions to the Navy’s ship-naming rules, particularly for the purpose of naming a ship for a person when the rule for...

title: Navy Columbia (SSBN-826) Class Ballistic Missile Submarine Program: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The Navy’s Columbia (SSBN-826) class ballistic missile submarine (SSBN) aims to design and build a class of 12 new SSBNs to replace the Navy’s current force of 14 aging Ohio-class SSBNs. Since 2013, the Navy has consistently identified the Columbia-class program as the Navy’s top priority program. The Navy procured the first Columbia-class boat in FY2021 and the second in FY2024. The Navy wants to procure the third...
